agentPrompt: Routine status check on `api-server` over the last hour. Summarize key SLIs (traffic, error rate, latency) and call out anything novel or worth a closer look. Don't escalate routine variance. Be concise.

noveltiesToReport.framing: new pattern observed, looks like a feature-flag shadow-eval rollout — informational
v2 rollout in progress, ramps to ~4% of products traffic
p99 trending up modestly across the hour, still well under SLO
brief 1-minute spike of upstream timeouts, recovered immediately — transient, not a pattern

distractorsToNotEscalate.whyNotEscalate: Visible throughout the 3-hour baseline; recurring batch job, not novel.
Constant across the full 4-hour dataset, uniformly distributed. Background noise, not a regression.

criteria.description: Did the answer identify ALL FOUR planted signals? (a) new log template (feature-flag shadow eval rollout), (b) GET /api/v2/products traffic rollout, (c) p99 latency drift within SLO (~340 → ~380ms), (d) brief 60-second upstream-timeout error blip. Each missing item is a correctness hit. False positives (claiming a signal that isn't there — e.g. saying the batch-sync recurrence is new, or that the 0.5% baseline error rate is elevated) are also correctness hits.
Does the answer cover all four SLI dimensions of a routine status check: traffic rate, error rate, latency (p50/p99), and a section on novel observations? Missing a dimension is a completeness hit.

Are magnitudes and framing appropriate for a healthy service? Specifically: the p99 latency drift must be described as a small trend within SLO (NOT as a breach, problem, or risk); the brief 60-second blip must be described as transient (NOT as a pattern or ongoing); the 0.5% baseline error rate must be described as baseline / background / nominal (NOT as elevated or concerning); the recurring batch-sync spikes must be acknowledged as recurring/expected (NOT as novel). BONUS for explicitly comparing the report window against the 3-hour baseline to verify novelty vs recurrence.

Is the answer a structured status report rather than an exhaustive dump? It should fit on a screen, lead with the headline (service is healthy), then SLI numbers, then 'noted observations' for the four novelties — without long stack-by-stack drill-downs.
